mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in the unemployment rate below 5.0 percent signals labor market normalization, which boosts consumer confidence and aggregate demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative or zero forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances over a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts combined with expanding mortgage debt outstanding indicate synchronized credit and construction demand, which amplifies wealth effects and corporate revenue growth, lifting equity prices over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal shows a negative or statistically insignificant correlation with 60-day forward SPX returns across three consecutive business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth reflects tightening supply constraints and rising capacity utilization, which historically precedes margin expansion and positive equity returns over a one-week trading window.
regime_hint: tight labor market with capacity constraints
failure_condition: Industrial production growth decouples from equity returns, producing a negative Rank IC exceeding -0.05 over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly real GDP growth rate exceeding 2.5 percent confirms robust economic expansion, which validates higher corporate profit forecasts and triggers positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are flat or negative in more than 60% of quarters where GDP growth exceeds the 2.5 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous rises in consumer price inflation and the federal funds rate indicate an aggressive tightening cycle that compresses equity valuations and suppresses risk appetite, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The combined inflation-rate signal fails to predict negative equity returns in more than 50% of historical episodes matching the regime.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls signals sustained labor demand, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: The direction of payroll changes fails to predict the sign of 5-day forward SPX returns with a hit rate above 52% over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in mortgage debt outstanding exceeding 5 percent signals credit market contraction, which restricts household liquidity and dampens equity risk premiums, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally or remain flat in more than 65% of instances following the specified credit contraction threshold.
